questions.question: Two threads are executing a program with four iterations. The threads execute the same code. What is the effect on performance in this scenario? Consider the overhead of context switching.

A task with four iterations is divided into two threads. If each thread runs for two iterations, what is the total time taken by the two threads to complete the task? Assume that the time taken by each thread to execute one iteration is 't'.

Two threads are executing a program with four iterations. The threads are waiting for each other to complete. What is the effect on the total execution time of the program? Consider the overhead of synchronization.
